mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of accommodative monetary policy reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate earnings growth, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during easing cycles, with Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter financial conditions, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >60% of months with rising CPI, or inflation shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll additions above the 200,000 threshold confirm robust economic momentum and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns are negative in >50% of months following strong payroll prints, or threshold loses predictive accuracy after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real personal consumption expenditures where consumption outpaces GDP signals strong household balance sheets and discretionary spending, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Consumption-GDP spread shows zero correlation with forward returns, or joint signal fails to beat buy-and-hold in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle phases indicates capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that suppress corporate output and earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative signal coincides with positive forward returns in >60% of late-cycle observations, or industrial production loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy.
